characters.name: Abigail Fuller
characters.description: A grown up Abigail (Melora Hardin) with Sam as Will Kinman.Series informationSeriesQuantum LeapAbigail Fulleris the daughter ofSheriff Clayton FullerandLaura Fullerand the mother ofSammy Jo Fuller, whom she conceived withSamuel Beckett, while Sam was leaping asWill Kinman, Abigail's fiancée in the episode "For Your Love". Abigail is played by actressKimberly Cullumas a child and actressMelora Hardinas an adult. Sam as Sheriff Clayton Fuller, Abigail's father, in scene with maid Marie Billings (Fran Bennett) and a young Abigail (Kimberly Cullum) in "The Trilogy:Part I". Sam has to save Abigail not once, but three different times (first into a leap as her father,Clayton Fuller, second as her fiancée,Will Kinmanand third as her lawyer,Lawrence "Larry" Stanton III–"Trilogy", parts 1, 2, & 3 respectively). When he leaps into themanshe is set to marry, he falls in love with her himself and fathers a daughter, which turns out to beSammy Jo. Sammy Jo would have high IQ and a photographic memory, just like Sam. Later, Al reveals that Sammy Jo is to become a part ofProject Quantum Leapin order to find a way to bring him back. The part of Abigail as an adult is played by actressMelora Hardinin For Your Love: Part II and The Last Door: Part III. The part of Abigail as a child is played by actressKimberly Cullum(who also plays daughterSammy Join Part III) inTrilogy: Part I.

characters.name: Al the Bartender
characters.description: Albert BellisarioAl the Bartender with Sam BeckettGeneral InformationOccupation / CareerBartender/Bar OwnerSeries informationSeriesQuantum Leap (1989) Albert "Al" Bellisariois a mysterious bartender Dr. Sam Beckett met in thefinal episode of Quantum Leap. The part of Al the Bartender is played by actorBruce McGill. He is heavily implied to be the one behind Sam's leaps (though he claims that Sam is the one behind Sam's leaps) and is also assumed to be God Himself. Al was based onDonald P. Bellisario's father Al Bellisario. Don said that since his dad created him and Don createdQuantum Leap, it made sense to portray him as a God-like figure in the QL universe.

characters.name: Albert Calavicci
characters.actor_name: Dean StockwellJamie Walters(Young Al in the Season 4 episodeA Leap For Lisa)
characters.description: Al CalavicciAl CalavicciGeneral InformationFull nameRear Admiral Albert Calavicci, USN, retiredAKAAlAffiliationUnited State NavyProject Quantum LeapOccupation / CareerFormer Naval fighter pilot during the Vietnam War, flag officer, former POW in Vietnam, and part ofProject Quantum LeapFamilyBeth Calavicci(wife)4 daughters (with Beth, after altered events in lastMirror Image)5 wives, including Beth, before timeline changeTrudy Calavicci(sister)Janis Calavicci(daughter)Biographical informationDate of birthJune 15, 1934Place of birthPhiladelphia, PA, U.S.Date of death2021Series informationActor(s)Dean StockwellJamie Walters(Young Al in the Season 4 episodeA Leap For Lisa)SeriesQuantum LeapFirst appearanceGenesis (episode)Last appearanceMirror Image 5 wives, including Beth, before timeline changeTrudy Calavicci(sister)Janis Calavicci(daughter) Rear AdmiralAlbert 'Al' Calavicciis the project observer at Quantum Leap, who appears toSamuel Beckettas a hologram. The part of Al is played by veteran stage and film/TV actorDean Stockwell, who won a Golden Globe Award in 1990 for his supporting role as Al.

characters.description: AliaEvil leaper Alia (Renee Coleman) on "Quantum Leap"Series informationSeriesQuantum LeapFirst appearance3 episodes, beginning with"Deliver Us From Evil"(#5.7) Aliais a femaleEvil LeaperthatSamfirst met in the episode titled"Deliver Us From Evil". The part of Alia is played byRenée Coleman. She is the evil leaper counterpart toSam. Nothing is known about Alia's past or her background, or even which time period she is originally from.

characters.name: Sister Angela
characters.description: Sister AngelaMichelle Joyner as Sister Angela in "The Right Hand of God" in Season 1 (ep.#4).General InformationOccupation / CareerNun at in Sacremento, California, parish in 1974Series informationSeriesQuantum Leap (1989)First appearance"The Right Hand of God"(Season 1, episode #4)Sister Angela, a nun, is a character who appears in theSeason 1episode ofQuantum Leaptitled"The Right Hand of God". The part of Sister Angela is played in the episode byMichelle Joyner. Sam leaps into the body ofClarence "Kid" Cody, a boxer, who usually on the take to either take dives or win in fixed matches for local boxing promoterJake Edwards, who pressures him into doing so, until a chance meeting with Sister Angela, who agrees to become his new manager in return for the promise that he, who is about to fight in an upcoming match for a city heavyweight boxing championship belt, would donate part of his winnings to help Angela and the other nuns at the local parish where she works at to build a new chapel to replace the present one, which is ill-equipped to house and serve the needs of the poor in the community, as well as in a state of disrepair. Sister Angela, who befriended leapee Clarence's former manager, a wealthy parishioner who had recently died, and who had bequeathed her and her sisters at the parish the rights to manage Kid Cody; when Sam meets her and learns of the deal, it seems to him and Al, that it's going to be an uphill battle and a longshot of getting Sam, who admits that he's out of shape and doesn't know how to box at all, in shape and proficient enough in his boxing skills to defeat his opponent in the upcouming bout, as Sam is able to get his leapee's trainerMr. Gomez(Alex Colon), who has trained him in the bouts he's thrown for Jake, and vowed, after the last bought Cody had, which Sam leaped into and won with a knockout, was their last fight together, to hang on and trains him to try to actually win the match.

Sister Angela, who befriended leapee Clarence's former manager, a wealthy parishioner who had recently died, and who had bequeathed her and her sisters at the parish the rights to manage Kid Cody; when Sam meets her and learns of the deal, it seems to him and Al, that it's going to be an uphill battle and a longshot of getting Sam, who admits that he's out of shape and doesn't know how to box at all, in shape and proficient enough in his boxing skills to defeat his opponent in the upcouming bout, as Sam is able to get his leapee's trainerMr. Gomez(Alex Colon), who has trained him in the bouts he's thrown for Jake, and vowed, after the last bought Cody had, which Sam leaped into and won with a knockout, was their last fight together, to hang on and trains him to try to actually win the match.

characters.name: Angelita (Sam's Guardian Angel)
characters.description: Angelita (Guardian Angel)Sam, as NYC cabbie Max, meets Angelina (Liz Torres), a guardian angel assigned to he and his leapee, in the Season 4 episode "It's A Wonderful Leap".General InformationFull nameAngelita Carmen Guadalupe Cecelia JimenezDescriptionLate former stage performer who God sent back to Earth as a Guardian angel to protect Sam and Max Greeman, the NYC cabbie he leaps into from death in the attempt to secure an expensive medallion, a prize in a contest ran by his boss, who attempts to use it as a scamOccupation / CareerLounge singer/stage performer during her lifetimeBiographical informationDate of birth????, perhaps 1885-1895Date of death1928, after slipping and falling off the stage while performingSeries informationSeriesQuantum LeapFirst appearance"It's A Wonderful Leap"in Season 4Last appearanceN/A (one time appearance) Angelita Carmen Guadalupe Cecelia Jimenez, or, as in the episode, simply referred to asAngelita, was a character who appears in the Season 4 episode of the NBC-TV seriesQuantum Leap, in the episode"It's A Wonderful Leap". The part of Angelita is played by actress/entertainer and singerLiz Torres. Angelita is a "guardian angel" who, in life, was once a stage performer on Broadway in the 1920s, who was sent back to New York City by God in 1958, to oversee the events in the life of a cabby namedMax Greenman(Ross Partridge), whom Sam leaps into, is trying to earn enough money to open his own cab service, along with his fatherLenny(Jerry Adler). Max is hoping to win his own cab driver's medallion in the contest his employer is running to use as money to start their taxi service. Angelita is not only assigned to protect Max from getting shot in a robbery attempt, but also is assigned to protect Dr. Beckett, whom she can see as the leaper into the life of Max.

Max is hoping to win his own cab driver's medallion in the contest his employer is running to use as money to start their taxi service. Angelita is not only assigned to protect Max from getting shot in a robbery attempt, but also is assigned to protect Dr. Beckett, whom she can see as the leaper into the life of Max.

characters.name: Archie Necaise
characters.description: Deputy Archie Necaise (Bob Hamilton, left, in mirror) and leaper Sam in early mirror image scene in the Season 4 episode "Hurricane"Series informationSeriesQuantum Leap Deputy Sheriff Archie Necaisewas a character who appeared in the Season 4 episode ofQuantum LeapTV series titled"Hurricane". The character Archie, as the leapee, is seen in an uncredited cameo appearance by Bob Hamilton. Archie, the deputy sheriff of the small Mississippi town called Jackson's Point, whom Dr. Beckett leaps into in the episode"Hurricane", andCissy Davis. a local area Red Cross Nurse and his girlfriend, are helping to evacuate Archie's ex-girlfriendLisa(played byTracy Kolis) as Hurricane Camille, which devastated the Gulf Coast region at that time, is set to hit the town. Al reveals that in the original course of events in August 1969, Cissy was killed by debris that night when she returns home. Sam and Cissy try to evacuate a ‘hurricane party’ but they refuse to leave as the host of the party is a hippie who says his apartment has recently been made into a cooperative, which means he is now the legal owner and cannot be forced out of his private property. Sam realizes that while this may be foolhardy, the partygoers broke no law and heads back to the shelter. Before leaving, the host sees Sam's true self, but chalks it up to clouded judgment, remarking "one moment you got black hair, the next you got blonde hair". Sam looks in a mirror to see Archie.

characters.name: Arnold Watkins
characters.description: Arnold WatkinsSeries informationSeriesQuantum LeapFirst appearance"Return of The Evil Leaper"in Season 5 Arnold Watkinswas a college student who pretended to be the "Midnight Marauder". He was saved bySamfrom dying. Sam has to persuade a fraternity to stop using chicken races as a part of their hazing ceremonies, while Al tries to convince Arnold to stop trying to get himself killed in retaliation for his parents' deaths twelve years earlier. Sam seeing Arnold in the mirror.

characters.name: Barbara Whitmore
characters.description: Barbara WhitmoreBarbara Whitmo (Liz Vassey) in "Goodbye, Norma Jean" episode in Season 5 of the NBC-TV series "Quantum Leap".General InformationOccupation / Careeractress, seductressSeries informationSeriesQuantum Leap (1989)First appearance"Goodbye Norma Jean"(Season 5) Barbara Whitmoreappears in the Season 5 episode of the NBC-TV seriesQuantum Leaptitled"Goodbye Norma Jean". She is portrayed byLiz Vassey. Barbara, whose real name Mary Jones Swallel, was born in Pasadena, California and was a childhood actor at 12 years old. Her acting career had finished and she was out of money. She decided to pretend to be Marilyn Monroe's gopher to get close to famous directors and producers to get back on her feet.

characters.name: Irene Basch
characters.description:
Irene BaschTerri Hanauer as Irene Basch in "Thou Shalt Not..." in Season 2 (ep.#7).General InformationOccupation / CareerHousewife, Los Angeles, CA, in 1964Series informationSeriesQuantum Leap (1989)First appearance"Thou Shalt Not..."(Season 2) Irene Baschappears in theSeason 2episode ofQuantum Leaptitled"Thou Shalt Not...". The part of Irene is played in the episode byTerri Hanauer. When Sam, who leaps intoRabbi David Basch, who has just conducted the Bat Mitzvah ofKaren, Irene's daughter (played byLindsay Fisher), he finds a quite upset Joe, Irene's husband, who refuses to dance with her, as she dances with a friend,Bert Glasserman(Russ Tamblyn), whom he, as with possibly even his brother David suspects that one or the other of having an affair with. 
In the original timeline of events before Sam's leap into the situation, Irene had a one-night stand which gets found out and destroys the family, something Karen never gets over.

characters.name: Bebe
characters.description: Sami Chester as Bebe Bebeis a character who appears in theSeason 3episode ofQuantum Leap(the original series) titled"Black On White On Fire"(episode #38). The part of Bebe is played in the episode bySami Chester. As the episode begins,Dr. Beckett, who has leaped into the summer of 1965 Watts, CA, into oneRay Harper, a black medical student, is getting pummled upon by Bebe (Sami Chester), who's a buddy of his brotherLonnie Harper, who's a sort of community activist, who's views on race sharply clash and are at odds with Ray, who is dating a white girl,Susan Brewster(Corie Henninger) fellow medical student whose father,Paul Brewster(Marc Alaimo) is a Captain on the Watts PD. Along with Lonnie, Bebe, who shows not only contempt for Ray dating Susan, but also distrusts her dating Ray, as does a onePapa David(Ron Taylor) another friend of Lonnie's, as Lonnie says it's only because she wants to showcase Ray as "a trophy among her white liberal friends". It takes their mother,Mama Harper(CCH Pounder), who threatens Bebe and Papa David with a shotgun, for Bebe to release Ray from his assault.

characters.name: Ben Song
characters.description: Ben SongGeneral InformationAffiliationProject Quantum LeapOccupation / CareerScientist/Quantum PhysicistFamilyUnnamed mother †Unnamed fatherBiographical informationMarital statusAddison Augustine(ex-fiancee)Hannah Carson(ex-girlfriend)Series informationActor(s)Raymond LeeSeriesQuantum Leap(revival)First appearanceS01E01:July 13th, 1985Last appearancen/a - main character Unnamed father Hannah Carson(ex-girlfriend) Ben is an introvert who doesn't like parties or public speaking. Ben is decent to a fault, nearly always seeing the good in people and seeking to change their hearts. He also has a reckless streak, often endangering himself with a poorly thought-out plan in order the maximize the good he achieves in a leap.

characters.name: Beth Calavicci
characters.description: Beth CalavicciBeth Calavicci.General InformationFull nameBeth CalavicciOccupation / CareerNaval Officer and Burn Ward Nurse (1969)FamilyJanis Calavicci(daughter)3 unnamed daughtersBiographical informationMarital statusAlbert Calavicci(husband)Herbert "Magic" Williams(boyfriend)Series informationActor(s)Susan DiolSeriesQuantum Leap(original)Quantum Leap(revival)First appearanceM.I.A. Beth Calavicci(played bySusan Diol) isAl's wife/widow, and the only woman Al has ever really loved. Beth was a US Navy nursing officer while Al was a pilot. They married in 1961, but due to their differing duty assignments they only cohabited for a total of 2 years before the end of the decade. On Al's second tour of duty in Vietnam, he was shot down and taken captive; Beth had no way of knowing if he was still alive. While serving at the Naval Hospital in San Diego in April 1969, a very lonely Beth met Dirk Simon and fell in love.

characters.description: Bobo in "The Wrong Stuff" in Season 4Series informationSeriesQuantum LeapFirst appearance"The Wrong Stuff"in Season 4Bobowas # 52 chimpanzee in the chimponaut corps.Samleaped into him in the Season 4 episode,"The Wrong Stuff", on January 24, 1961, with a mission to get him to the space program. He failed in the beginning, but managed to save Bobo anyway. After that,Alpredicted that Bobo wold start a family with another chimp in the chimponaut corps,Corey.

characters.name: Boss Cooley
characters.description: Boss CooleyGeneral InformationOccupation / CareerPrison Guard, Talawaga County Prison, Talwaga, MississippiSeries informationActor(s)J.C. QuinnSeriesQuantum LeapFirst appearance"Unchained"(Season 4)Last appearanceN/A (one-time appearance) Boss Cooleyis the corrupt prison guard of Talawaga County Prison, in Talawaga, Mississippi, who appears in theSeason 4episode"Unchained"(episode #10). The part of Boss Cooley is played in the episode byJ.C. Quinn. Boss Cooley is in hot pursuit of escaped prisonerChance Terence Cole(leapee played byMark Kemble), an inmate whomSam Beckettleaps into in 1956, who is serving time, doing nine months for petty theft, who is handcuffed into a black man,Jasper "Jazz" Boone(played byBasil Wallace), a black man doing 15 years for armed robbery who, according to Al, has been unjustly imprisoned after a jewelry store robbery, as a rash of them had been occuring in the area.

characters.name: Susan Brewster
characters.description:
Susan Brewster'is a character who appears in theSeason 3episode ofQuantum Leap(the original series)titled"Black On White On Fire". The part of Susan Brewster is portrayed in the episode by actressCorie Henninger. In 1965 Los Angeles,Samleaps into a oneRay Harper(Garon Grigsby), a promising African-American medical student who has the opportunity for internship at a Boston hospital. Ray currently lives in Watts and it's the eve of the 1965 race riots.
He's engaged to Susan, a white girl who's a fellow medical student, and faces not only the distrust of her father, (Police Capt. Paul Brewster(Marc Alaimo), who questions his daughter's reason for dating Ray, but also the wrath and contempt of his older brother Lonnie (Gregory Millar), a sort of community activist, who's militant views strongly clash and are at odds with Ray's, and also Lonnie's friends, who more vehemently, disapprove of he dating Susan. Al tells him that his purpose is to keep Ray and Susan together but there is an outside chance that Susan might die in the riots. WhenBebe(Sami Chester) one of Lonnie's buddies who disapproves of Ray's involvement with a white girl, begins beating on Ray, Lonnie's mother,Mama Harper(CCH Pounder) saves leapee Ray and Sam, as she threatens Lonnie's friends as she wields a shotgun. Mama Harper is much more understanding and accepting of Ray's relationship with Susan.

characters.name: Buddy Wright
characters.description: Buddy WrightBuddy Wright in "What Price Gloria?"General InformationOccupation / CareerAutomoblie designerSeries informationSeriesQuantum LeapFirst appearance"What Price Gloria?" Buddy Wrightwas a successful automobile designer who appears in the episode ofQuantum Leaptitled"What Price Gloria?". Nothing is known of Buddy Wright's past or childhood. At some point he became a leading car designer who hired beautiful secretaries more for their pulchritude than their credentials. He was especially interested inSamantha, his best secretary and often tried to start something with her. Buddy was a chauvanistic type, a manipulative person who knew how to trick and manipulate women. He talked softly and with determination. Buddy was also very deceiving.
characters.personality_section: Buddy was a chauvanistic type, a manipulative person who knew how to trick and manipulate women. He talked softly and with determination. Buddy was also very deceiving.

characters.name: Capt. Cooper
characters.description: Captain CooperCapt. Cooper (Scott Hoxby) with Sam Beckett (Scott Bakula) in the episode "Ghost Ship".General InformationDescriptionFormer US Navy pilot who had a experience in the Bermuda Triangle in WWII while serving as a fighter pilot who still suffers from post-traumatic stress disorder and who must transport a married couple through the Triangle again in 1956Occupation / CareerNavy pilot and head of air taxi serviceSeries informationSeriesQuantum Leap (1989)First appearance"Ghost Ship"' in Season 4Last appearanceN/A (one time appearance)Capt. Cooper(Scott Hoxby) a Navy pilot, is also the head pilot of a air taxi in theQuantum Leapepisode titled"Ghost Ship"in Season 4 of the series. The part of Cooper is played by actorScott Hoxby. Cooper is the head pilot of an air taxi that is transporting he, his co-pilot Sam asEddie Brackett, his wifewendy, (Kimberly Foster) and two young newlywedsGrant Cutter, Jr.(Kurt Deutsch) and wifeMichele(Carla Gugino), who is very sick with appendicitis. Cooper, who once had a previous experience in the Bermuda Triangle while serving as a WWII fighter pilot, still suffers from post-traumatic stress disorder, which makes Sam's task a difficult one in trying to persuade him to fly on to Bermuda, through the heart of the Triangle, instead of turning back to land in Northern Virginia, so that they could get treatment for Michelle's appendicitis. In 1944 Cooper located and sunk seven U-Boats in the Bermuda Triangle and on his last mission in November, the Shark Squadron flight he was leading were lost in a storm with the three other pilots killed.  He was rescued by the tanker liberty ship Michael Z eight days later, but claimed that he had spent seven of those days aboard the collier USS Cyclops before it was torpedoed and sunk with all hands except himself making him the sole survivor for the second time.
